sglang.title: Revert removing the unused imports
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Reverts #378
Some public APIs are broken, such as from sglang import RuntimeEndpoint, this is because the former PR falsely removed these imports.
The public API should follow the format of __all__=[...].

vllm.title: [Core] Some simplification of WorkerWrapper changes
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
I am still catching up with reviews and only now looking at #4024 which was merged recently, because it impacts #3466 and #3763 that I need to rebase.
These are some minor changes to fix typing, reduce verbosity, reduce list allocations/slicing when running workers, remove some redundant code, etc.

vllm.title: [Core] Scheduling optimization 2
vllm.author: rkooo567
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds 2 optimization
I found the main overhead is coming from get_max_num_running_seqs because it needs to create a list of sequences.
This adds 2 optimizations

If status is None, get_num_seqs doesn't have to create a list.
If we already know num_seqs token budget is updated before calling s

vllm.title: [Speculative decoding 7/9] Speculative decoding end-to-end correctness tests.
vllm.author: cadedaniel
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds e2e correctness tests for speculative decoding. It is PR 7/9 in the speculative decoding open sourcing plan.
The E2E correctness tests verify that the generated output of a sequence with speculative decoding is equal to the generated output without speculative decoding when temperature

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fixing max token error message for openai compatible server
vllm.author: jgordley
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR addresses a bug where the error message is inaccurate when the user makes a ChatCompletionRequest with no max_tokens sent.
Example behavior:
from openai import OpenAI
client = OpenAI(
    base_url="http://localhost:8000/v1",
    api_key="token-abc123",
)

message_over_context_length = "Hello

vllm.title: [Core][Logging] Add last frame information for better debugging
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt: When the process hangs after returning to a function, it is unclear where is the process, and users have to analyze the call chain manually. This PR adds the from and to information directly in the log file, so that debugging issues like #4277 will be easier.

vllm.title: [CI] Add ccache for wheel builds job
vllm.author: simon-mo
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This should hopefully improves the wheel building job's latency.

vllm.title: AQLM CUDA support
vllm.author: jaemzfleming
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
SUMMARY:
Supports AQLM compressed inference, see
https://github.com/Vahe1994/AQLM
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2401.06118.pdf
Optimized supported formats are 1x16 and 2x8. Tensor parallelism is supported. Only CUDA kernels are provided.  Formats other than 1x16 and 2x8 will run but at lower performance.
Al

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Frontend] Raise exception when file-like chat template fails to be opened
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Currently, when the value provided to --chat-template looks like a file path, but the file fails to be opened, the filename is read as the literal chat template, resulting in much confusion as to why the model fails to generate output.
This PR adds a check for this case so that the user is notified

vllm.title: [Kernel] FP8 support for MoE kernel / Mixtral
vllm.author: pcmoritz
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR is the first step towards fixing #3208
It implements dynamic per-tensor scaling (see #4118), so users do not need to compute activation scales on a calibration dataset and they also don't need to convert their model checkpoints. It is enough to specify the quantization="fp8" argument. You ca

vllm.title: [Core][Distributed] use cpu/gloo to initialize pynccl
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
nccl initialization requires broadcasting a unique id, which lives in cpu memory. previously, we only have one nccl backend process group, so we have to move the unique id to gpu, broadcast it, and then move it back to cpu.
After #3904 , we always have a cpu/gloo backend, so we don't need to move th

vllm.title: [CI][Build] change pynvml to nvidia-ml-py
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Quote from nv team:

The pynvml library is effectively a mirror of whatever is released by nvidia-ml-py (which is managed by the "real" NVML team). We don't do any development or bug-fixes in pynvml, so the official recommendation is to use nvidia-ml-py directly.

vllm.title: [Misc] Reduce supported Punica dtypes
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR reduces the supported dtype combinations in Punica to reduce the binary size.
